name: CFA Level 1
description: Globally recognized finance certification covering investment analysis and portfolio management.
cost_source: CFA Institute Official (May 2026) — $1,140 early registration, $1,490 standard. One-time $350 enrollment fee eliminated April 29 2025.
exam_format: 180 multiple-choice questions across 2 sessions (90 questions per session, each 2hr 15min)
passing_score_note: CFA Institute does not publish a fixed passing score. The Minimum Passing Score (MPS) is set by the Board of Governors after each exam sitting.
pass_rate_source: CFA Institute historical data — Level 1 pass rate typically ~40%
renewal_method: No renewal required once all 3 levels passed and CFA charter awarded
prerequisites: Bachelor's degree OR in final year of bachelor's program OR 4,000 hours of professional experience

name: Bloomberg Market Concepts
description: Self-paced e-learning course on financial markets using Bloomberg Terminal data and tools.
cost_source: Bloomberg for Education portal (May 2026) — free via university Bloomberg Terminal access
exam_format: Self-paced e-learning with embedded quizzes — no standalone timed exam. Certificate awarded on course completion.
passing_score_note: No formal pass/fail score — completion-based certificate. No published pass rate.
renewal_method: No renewal required — lifetime certificate on completion

name: FRM Part 1 (Financial Risk Manager)
description: GARP's flagship risk management certification covering quantitative analysis, financial markets, valuation, and risk models. Part 1 of 2 required for the full FRM designation.
cost_source: GARP Official (June 2026) — $400 one-time enrollment fee + $600–$800 exam fee depending on registration timing
passing_score_note: GARP does not publish a fixed passing score — results reported as pass/fail with quartile performance breakdown
pass_rate_source: GARP historical data — Part 1 pass rate typically 45–58%
renewal_cost_note: Annual CPD (Continuing Professional Development) required — no monetary renewal fee but 40 CPD hours per 2-year cycle
prerequisites: No formal prerequisites for Part 1 — 2 years work experience required before charter awarded

name: CFP (Certified Financial Planner)
description: The gold standard for personal financial planning, covering retirement, tax, estate, investment, and insurance planning. Required or strongly preferred for financial advisor roles in the US.
cost_source: CFP Board Official (June 2026) — early registration $825, standard $925
exam_format: Multiple choice, 170 questions across 2 sessions (3 hours each)
passing_score_note: CFP Board does not publish a fixed passing score — pass/fail determined after each exam administration
pass_rate_source: CFP Board historical data — November 2025 pass rate was 64%
renewal_cost_note: Biennial renewal fee of $355 + 30 CE hours every 2 years
prerequisites: Bachelor's degree + 6,000 hours professional experience (or 4,000 hours apprenticeship) + CFP Board-registered education program

name: FMVA (Financial Modeling & Valuation Analyst)
description: Practical financial modeling and valuation certification covering 3-statement models, DCF, M&A, LBO, and Excel-based financial analysis. Widely adopted in investment banking and corporate finance.
cost_source: CFI Official (June 2026) — Self-Study $497/year, Full Immersion $847/year; subscription model includes all courses
exam_format: 50 multiple-choice questions + Excel-based case studies, open-book, 3 hours
passing_score_note: 70% required (35/50 questions). 30-day waiting period between attempts.
pass_rate_source: Community estimate — CFI does not publish official pass rates
renewal_cost_note: Certification does not expire — active subscription required to take exam

name: CAIA Level 1 (Chartered Alternative Investment Analyst)
description: Global certification for alternative investment professionals covering hedge funds, private equity, real assets, structured products, and risk management.
cost_source: CAIA Association Official (June 2026) — $400 enrollment + $850–$1,150 exam fee depending on registration date
passing_score_note: CAIA does not publish a fixed MPS — pass/fail determination varies by exam administration
pass_rate_source: Community estimate — CAIA does not publish official pass rates
renewal_cost_note: Annual membership fee of $550 required to maintain charter
prerequisites: Bachelor's degree OR 1 year of professional experience in finance

name: CPA (Certified Public Accountant)
description: The most recognised accounting credential globally, required for public accounting roles in the US. Covers auditing, financial accounting, regulation, and business environment. One of the highest-ROI certifications in finance.
cost_source: AICPA/NASBA Official (June 2026) — 4 exam sections at ~$250 each + state licensing fees vary
exam_format: 4 exam sections (AUD, FAR, REG, BAR/ISC/TCP) — multiple choice + task-based simulations per section

exam_parts.name: Auditing and Attestation (AUD)
exam_parts.description: Covers auditing procedures, professional responsibilities, ethics, and general principles.

exam_parts.name: Financial Accounting and Reporting (FAR)
exam_parts.description: Covers financial statements, transactions, GAAP standards, and reporting requirements.

exam_parts.name: Regulation (REG)
exam_parts.description: Covers federal taxation, business law, ethics, and professional responsibilities.

exam_parts.name: Discipline (BAR, ISC, or TCP)
exam_parts.description: Allows candidates to choose one specialized track: Business Analysis (BAR), Info Systems (ISC), or Tax Compliance (TCP).

passing_score_note: 75 on a 0–99 scale required per section. Scaled score, not percentage.
pass_rate_source: AICPA historical data — individual section pass rates typically 45–55%
renewal_cost_note: Annual state licensing fees vary ($50–$200) + 40 CPE hours per year required
prerequisites: 150 credit hours of education (typically bachelor's + additional coursework) + 1 year work experience (varies by state)

name: CFA ESG Investing Certificate
description: Validates knowledge of ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) factors in investment analysis and portfolio management. Fastest-growing finance certification as sustainable investing becomes mainstream.
passing_score_note: Approximately 70% required — CFA Institute does not publish an exact threshold

name: CMA (Certified Management Accountant)
description: The premier certification for management accounting and corporate financial management. Covers financial planning, performance, control, and decision analysis.
cost_source: IMA Official (2026) — including enrollment, membership, and 2 exam parts fee
exam_format: 2 exam parts (multiple choice + essay questions per part)

exam_parts.name: Part 1: Financial Planning, Performance, and Analytics
exam_parts.description: Covers external financial reporting decisions, planning, budgeting, forecasting, performance management, cost management, and internal controls.

exam_parts.name: Part 2: Strategic Financial Management
exam_parts.description: Covers financial statement analysis, corporate finance, decision analysis, risk management, investment decisions, and professional ethics.

passing_score_note: Passing score is 360 on a scale of 0 to 500 per part.
pass_rate_source: IMA historical average pass rates globally
renewal_cost_note: Annual IMA membership fee + 30 hours of CPE per year (including 2 hours of ethics)
prerequisites: Bachelor's degree from an accredited college/university + 2 years of continuous professional experience in management accounting or financial management

name: QuickBooks Certified User
description: Entry-level certification that validates proficiency in Intuit QuickBooks software, covering bookkeeping, payroll, invoicing, and reporting.
cost_source: Certiport Official (2026) — exam voucher cost, learning materials optional
exam_format: 50 questions, mix of multiple choice and simulation tasks
exam_parts: QuickBooks Desktop or Online User Exam
renewal_cost_note: No renewal required, though updating for newer software versions is recommended

name: CFA Level 2
description: The second level of the CFA charter program, focusing on asset valuation, application of investment tools, and complex analysis of financial reporting.
cost_source: CFA Institute Official (2026) — depending on early vs standard registration
exam_format: 88 item set questions (mini case studies with 4 multiple-choice questions each) across 2 sessions

exam_parts.name: CFA Level II Exam - Morning Session
exam_parts.description: First session of 44 item-set multiple-choice questions focusing on ethics, investment tools, and asset classes.

exam_parts.name: CFA Level II Exam - Afternoon Session
exam_parts.description: Second session of 44 item-set multiple-choice questions focusing on application of valuation models and portfolio analysis.

passing_score_note: CFA Institute sets a Minimum Passing Score (MPS) after each exam administration based on psychometric analysis.
pass_rate_source: CFA Institute historical average pass rate (~44-46%)
renewal_cost_note: No renewal required; progress directly to CFA Level 3

name: FRM Part 2 (Financial Risk Manager)
description: The final part of the FRM designation, focusing on the practical application of risk management concepts including market, credit, operational, and liquidity risk, as well as current financial issues.
cost_source: GARP Official (2026) — depending on early vs standard registration timing; enrollment fee paid in Part 1
passing_score_note: GARP does not publish a fixed passing score; results reported as pass/fail with quartile breakdown
renewal_cost_note: Annual CPD (Continuing Professional Development) required — no monetary fee but 40 CPD hours per 2-year cycle
